title_en: Methodology and Hadith Authentication in the Adhkar
content_en:
Al-Nawawi's methodology in al-Adhkar represents a careful balance between comprehensiveness and scholarly integrity. He was committed to providing Muslims with the widest possible range of authenticated prophetic supplications, while being honest about the varying levels of authentication for different formulas.

For each supplication or dhikr formula, Al-Nawawi typically cites the source — specifying which of the hadith collections contains the narration — and provides an assessment of its reliability. He uses the standard terminology of hadith criticism: sahih (sound), hasan (good), da'if (weak), and occasionally notes specific concerns about narrators or chains. This critical apparatus, integrated into what might otherwise be a simple anthology of supplications, reflects Al-Nawawi's formation as a hadith scholar.

Al-Nawawi's position on using weak hadiths in matters of spiritual merit and voluntary practice (fada'il al-a'mal) follows the majority view of classical scholars: that weak hadiths — not fabricated ones — may be cited for encouraging voluntary acts of worship and remembrance, provided that one does not present them as establishing specific obligations or claim specific numerical rewards that the hadith itself specifies. This position explains why al-Adhkar includes some hadiths that Al-Nawawi himself notes are weak.

The organization of al-Adhkar by time and circumstance reflects Al-Nawawi's practical purpose: to make the prophetic dhikr tradition accessible for daily use. Rather than organizing by topic in the manner of a fiqh text or by narrator in the manner of a musnad, he organized by the moments of daily life when Muslims need specific guidance. This organization has made the work function as a practical handbook that Muslims can use directly rather than merely study.

Al-Nawawi also provides explanatory notes on many of the supplications, explaining unusual words, identifying the circumstances of specific hadiths, and noting related supplications that complement the primary formula. These notes enhance the work's usefulness without burdening it with the kind of extended legal analysis found in his fiqh works.
title_id: Metodologi dan Autentikasi Hadis dalam Al-Adzkar
content_id:
Metodologi an-Nawawi dalam al-Adzkar mewakili keseimbangan yang cermat antara kelengkapan dan integritas ilmiah. Beliau berkomitmen untuk memberikan kepada kaum Muslim jangkauan seluas mungkin dari doa-doa kenabian yang sahih, sambil bersikap jujur tentang berbagai tingkat autentikasi untuk formula-formula yang berbeda.

Untuk setiap doa atau formula zikir, an-Nawawi biasanya mengutip sumbernya — menentukan koleksi hadis mana yang memuat riwayat tersebut — dan memberikan penilaian tentang keandalannya. Beliau menggunakan terminologi standar kritik hadis: sahih, hasan, daif, dan kadang-kadang mencatat kekhawatiran spesifik tentang perawi atau sanad. Aparatus kritis ini, yang diintegrasikan ke dalam apa yang sebaliknya mungkin hanya antologi doa sederhana, mencerminkan pembentukan an-Nawawi sebagai seorang ulama hadis.

Posisi an-Nawawi tentang penggunaan hadis-hadis daif dalam masalah keutamaan spiritual dan praktik sukarela (fadha'il al-amal) mengikuti pandangan mayoritas ulama klasik: bahwa hadis-hadis daif — bukan yang palsu — dapat dikutip untuk mendorong tindakan-tindakan ibadah dan zikir sukarela, asalkan seseorang tidak menyajikannya sebagai menetapkan kewajiban-kewajiban tertentu atau mengklaim pahala numerik spesifik yang disebutkan hadis itu sendiri. Posisi ini menjelaskan mengapa al-Adzkar menyertakan beberapa hadis yang an-Nawawi sendiri catat sebagai daif.

Organisasi al-Adzkar berdasarkan waktu dan keadaan mencerminkan tujuan praktis an-Nawawi: untuk membuat tradisi zikir kenabian dapat diakses untuk penggunaan sehari-hari. Daripada mengorganisir berdasarkan topik seperti cara teks fikih atau berdasarkan perawi seperti cara musnad, beliau mengorganisir berdasarkan momen-momen kehidupan sehari-hari di mana Muslim memerlukan panduan spesifik. Organisasi ini telah menjadikan karya tersebut berfungsi sebagai panduan praktis yang dapat digunakan Muslim secara langsung daripada sekadar dipelajari.

An-Nawawi juga memberikan catatan penjelasan tentang banyak doa, menjelaskan kata-kata yang tidak biasa, mengidentifikasi keadaan-keadaan hadis tertentu, dan mencatat doa-doa terkait yang melengkapi formula primer. Catatan-catatan ini meningkatkan kegunaan karya tanpa membebaninya dengan jenis analisis hukum panjang yang ditemukan dalam karya-karya fikihnya.
content_ar: النووي's methodology in al-Adhkar represents a careful balance between comprehensiveness and العلمي integrity. He was committed to providing المسلمون with the widest possible range of authenticated prophetic supplications, بينما being honest about the varying levels of authentication for different formulas. For each الدعاء or الذكر formula, النووي typically cites the source — specifying which of the الحديث collections contains the narration — and provides an assessment of its reliability. He uses the standard terminology of الحديث criticism: صحيح (sound), حسن (الخير), ضعيف (weak), and occasionally notes specific concerns about narrators or chains. This البالغ الأهمية apparatus, integrated into what might otherwise be a simple anthology of supplications, reflects النووي's formation as a الحديث scholar. النووي's position on using weak hadiths in matters of spiritual merit and voluntary practice (fada'il al-a'mal) follows the majority view of الكلاسيكي العلماء: that weak hadiths — not fabricated ones — may be cited for encouraging voluntary acts of worship and الذكر, بشرط أن one does not present them as establishing specific obligations or claim specific numerical rewards that the الحديث itself specifies. This position explains why al-Adhkar includes some hadiths that النووي himself notes are weak. The organization of al-Adhkar by time and circumstance reflects النووي's practical purpose: to make the prophetic الذكر التراث accessible for daily use. Rather than organizing by topic in the manner of a الفقه text or by narrator in the manner of a musnad, he organized by the moments of daily الحياة عندما المسلمون need specific الهداية. This organization has made the work function as a practical handbook that المسلمون can use directly rather than merely study. النووي أيضاً provides explanatory notes on many of the supplications, explaining unusual words, identifying the circumstances of specific hadiths, and noting related supplications that complement the الأساسي formula. These notes enhance the work's usefulness without burdening it with the kind of extended legal analysis found in his الفقه works.
